data.keyword: how to treat a minor burn

result.keyword: how to treat a minor burn

items.markdown:
To treat a minor (first-degree) burn, `immediately cool the area with cool—not cold—running water for 10 to 15 minutes. Gently remove jewelry or tight clothing, apply a thin layer of petroleum jelly, and cover it with a sterile, nonstick bandage. Take over-the-counter pain medication as needed`.[](https://www.mayoclinic.org/first-aid/first-aid-burns/basics/art-20056649) [[1]](https://www.mayoclinic.org/first-aid/first-aid-burns/basics/art-20056649)[[2]](https://medlineplus.gov/ency/patientinstructions/000662.htm)[[3]](https://www.aad.org/public/everyday-care/injured-skin/burns/treat-minor-burns)

For complete care instructions, follow these steps and precautions:

**What to Do:** 

- **Stop the burning process:** Move away from the heat source.
- **Remove tight items:** Gently take off rings, watches, or tight clothing before swelling sets in.
- **Apply moisturizer:** After cooling, you can apply petroleum jelly or aloe vera.
- **Manage pain:** Take over-the-counter pain relievers like ibuprofen or acetaminophen.
- **Protect blisters:** Do not pop blisters. They act as a natural barrier against infection. If a blister breaks on its own, gently clean it with mild soap and water, apply antibiotic ointment, and cover it.[](https://www.mayoclinic.org/first-aid/first-aid-burns/basics/art-20056649) [[1]](https://www.mayoclinic.org/first-aid/first-aid-burns/basics/art-20056649)[[2]](https://www.aad.org/public/everyday-care/injured-skin/burns/treat-minor-burns)[[3]](https://www.ameriburn.org/patients/burn-first-aid)[[4]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ETfGfP-XIkc&t=5)

**What to Avoid:** 

- **NO Ice:** Extreme cold from ice or ice water can cause further tissue damage.
- **NO Home Remedies:** Do not put butter, toothpaste, oils, or powders on the burn, as these trap heat and can cause infection.
- **NO Sticking:** Do not pull off any clothing that is stuck to the burned skin.[](https://www.mayoclinic.org/first-aid/first-aid-burns/basics/art-20056649) [[1]](https://www.mayoclinic.org/first-aid/first-aid-burns/basics/art-20056649)[[2]](https://medlineplus.gov/ency/patientinstructions/000662.htm)[[3]](https://www.ameriburn.org/patients/burn-first-aid)

Consult a doctor or visit an urgent care facility if the burn is larger than the palm of your hand, is located on the face, hands, feet, or groin, or if you notice signs of infection (such as pus, spreading redness, or fever).[](https://www.ameriburn.org/patients/burn-first-aid) [[1]](https://www.ameriburn.org/patients/burn-first-aid)[[2]](https://www.hcafloridahealthcare.com/healthy-living/blog/first-aid-tips-for-treating-minor-burns)[[3]](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ETfGfP-XIkc&t=5)For detailed first-aid guidelines, refer to the [Mayo Clinic Burn First Aid Guide](https://www.mayoclinic.org/first-aid/first-aid-burns/basics/art-20056649) or the [American Burn Association](https://www.ameriburn.org/patients/burn-first-aid).[[1]](https://www.uptodate.com/contents/treatment-of-minor-thermal-burns/print)[[2]](https://beverlyhillspediatrician.com/treating-minor-burns-silvadene/)

expanded_element.title: Minor burns - aftercare: MedlinePlus Medical Encyclopedia
expanded_element.description: You may put a thin layer of ointment, such as petroleum jelly or aloe vera, on the burn. The ointment does not need to have antibiotics in it. Some antibiotic ointments can cause an allergic reaction. Do not use cream, lotion, oil, cortisone, butter, or egg white.

items.title: How to treat a first-degree, minor burn
items.description: Cool the burn. Immediately immerse the burn in cool tap water or apply cold, wet compresses. Do this for about 10 minutes or until the pain subsides. Read more
highlighted: Immediately immerse the burn in cool tap water or apply cold, wet compresses

items.description: by J Hudspith · 2004 · Cited by 201 — The wound should be cleansed twice daily with mild diluted chlorohexidine solution. The burn should be covered with a bland ointment such as liquid paraffin. Read more

items.title: Burns and scalds
items.description: You should treat all burns and scalds straight away. Hold the burn or scald under cool running water for 15 to 30 minutes, or until the pain feels better.
highlighted: Hold the burn or scald under cool running water for 15 to 30 minutes
